title: System Health & Uptime
lede: Is the platform up? Now you never have to wonder. The System Health dashboard shows you live status, 90-day uptime, and per-service metrics for every part of iMash — from your voice agents to the database — all on one beautiful page.

steps.title: Open the System Health dashboard
steps.content:
iMash monitors itself around the clock and turns that telemetry into a status page built just for you. Here's where to find it:

  1. In the left sidebar, click System Health (in the top group, next to Overview, Team & Access and Audit). You can also go directly to /monitor.
  2. The page title reads System Health & Uptime. From top to bottom you'll see: a big status banner, a KPI strip, a Live Activity row, and then a service list on the left with the service grid (or a service's detail view) on the right.
💡 If you don't see System Health in your sidebar, your administrator has hidden it for your role — ask the account owner to enable it in your View Profile.

images.alt: System Health & Uptime dashboard with status banner, KPI strip, live activity and service cards
images.caption: The System Health dashboard: status banner, KPIs, live activity, and every monitored service in one view

images.alt: System Health & Uptime dashboard with status banner, KPI strip, live activity and service cards
images.caption: The System Health dashboard: status banner, KPIs, live activity, and every monitored service in one view

steps.title: Read the status banner (and the color legend)
steps.content:
The banner at the top answers the big question at a glance:

  • Overall status — All Systems Operational when everything is healthy. If anything is off, the banner changes to Some Systems Degraded, Partial System Outage or Major System Outage and the banner tint changes with it (green → amber → red).
  • Updated … ago — how fresh the data is. The dashboard refreshes itself every minute while the tab is open; click the small refresh icon to update immediately.
  • Uptime · last 90 days — the platform-wide uptime percentage on the right.
  • 1H / 6H / 24H / 7D — the time-range selector. It controls the metric charts across the whole page, so you can zoom from the last hour out to the last week.

Not sure what a color means? Click Legend in the top-right corner of the page to see all five states: Operational, Degraded, Partial Outage, Outage and Unknown.

images.alt: Status banner showing All Systems Operational, 90-day uptime and the time range selector
images.caption: The status banner: overall status, freshness, 90-day uptime, and the 1H/6H/24H/7D range selector

images.alt: Status banner showing All Systems Operational, 90-day uptime and the time range selector
images.caption: The status banner: overall status, freshness, 90-day uptime, and the 1H/6H/24H/7D range selector

images.alt: Status legend popover listing Operational, Degraded, Partial Outage, Outage and Unknown
images.caption: Click Legend to decode the status colors

images.alt: Status legend popover listing Operational, Degraded, Partial Outage, Outage and Unknown
images.caption: Click Legend to decode the status colors

steps.title: Check the four headline KPIs
steps.content:
Right under the banner, the KPI strip gives you the health story in four numbers:

  • Platform Uptime — overall uptime across the last 90 days.
  • Healthy Services — how many monitored services are operational right now (e.g. 22/22).
  • Incidents — the number of incidents recorded in the last 90 days.
  • Live Calls — how many of your calls are in progress at this very moment.
💡 Healthy Services is the fastest "is it me or the platform?" check. If it shows all services healthy but something feels wrong in your account, the issue is likely local to your setup — reach out to support with details.

images.alt: KPI strip showing Platform Uptime, Healthy Services, Incidents and Live Calls
images.caption: Four headline numbers: uptime, healthy services, incidents, and your live calls

images.alt: KPI strip showing Platform Uptime, Healthy Services, Incidents and Live Calls
images.caption: Four headline numbers: uptime, healthy services, incidents, and your live calls

steps.title: Watch your live activity
steps.content:
The Live Activity row is proof the platform is working for you right now — it's scoped to your own account, not the whole platform:

  • calls in progress — your calls happening at this moment (the icon pulses while calls are live).
  • calls today — your total call volume so far today.
  • messages today — your SMS/WhatsApp/email message volume so far today.

images.alt: Live Activity row with calls in progress, calls today and messages today
images.caption: Live Activity: your own calls and messages, updating in real time

images.alt: Live Activity row with calls in progress, calls today and messages today
images.caption: Live Activity: your own calls and messages, updating in real time

steps.title: Browse the monitored services
steps.content:
Below the KPIs, every monitored service gets its own card, organized into three groups (the same groups appear in the left rail):

  • Application — the parts you interact with: Automation, Call Recording, Core API, Dashboard, Realtime Media, Telephony and Voice Agents.
  • Infrastructure — what everything runs on: Cache & State, Database, Database Pooler, Network Gateway, Tracking Proxy, and more.
  • Platform Services — supporting services such as API Gateway, Authentication and Data API.

Each card packs a lot into a small space:

  • A status pill (Operational / Degraded / …).
  • Uptime · 24h with a sparkline of the selected time range.
  • A mini 30-day uptime bar — each block is one day; green means a clean day.
  • Instances (healthy/desired), CPU and Memory load.

The sticky rail on the left lists the same services with their live status dot and 24-hour uptime — use it to jump between Overview and any service without scrolling.

images.alt: Application service cards showing status, uptime sparkline, 30-day bar and resource metrics
images.caption: Service cards: status, 24h uptime sparkline, 30-day history and live resource usage

images.alt: Application service cards showing status, uptime sparkline, 30-day bar and resource metrics
images.caption: Service cards: status, 24h uptime sparkline, 30-day history and live resource usage

images.alt: Left service rail grouped into Application, Infrastructure and Platform Services
images.caption: The left rail: every service, its status dot, and 24h uptime — one click away

images.alt: Left service rail grouped into Application, Infrastructure and Platform Services
images.caption: The left rail: every service, its status dot, and 24h uptime — one click away

steps.title: Drill into a service
steps.content:
Want the full story on one service? Open its detail view:

  1. Click any service card in the grid, or click the service's name in the left rail (for example Voice Agents).
  2. The header shows the service name, its status pill, and how many instances are healthy (e.g. 2/2 instances healthy).
  3. Five stat tiles summarize the service:
    • Uptime · 90d — long-term uptime, with the 24-hour figure underneath.
    • Instances — healthy vs. desired copies of the service.
    • Restarts · 24h — container restarts in the last day (0 is what you want).
    • Avg CPU and Peak Memory — resource utilization for the selected time range.
  4. The Uptime — last 90 days bar shows one block per day, followed by the service's incident history — or a friendly No incidents in the last 90 days when the record is clean.
💡 Hover any day block in the uptime bar to see that day's exact uptime percentage.

images.alt: Voice Agents service detail with status, stat tiles and 90-day uptime bar
images.caption: A service detail view: status, instances, restarts, CPU/memory, and 90 days of uptime

images.alt: Voice Agents service detail with status, stat tiles and 90-day uptime bar
images.caption: A service detail view: status, instances, restarts, CPU/memory, and 90 days of uptime

images.alt: Uptime last 90 days bar with No incidents message
images.caption: One block per day — all green means 90 flawless days

images.alt: Uptime last 90 days bar with No incidents message
images.caption: One block per day — all green means 90 flawless days

steps.title: Explore the Resource Metrics charts
steps.content:
Below the uptime history, the Resource Metrics section charts how the service is performing over the selected time range:

  • CPU Usage and Memory Usage — load over time, with the current and average values in each chart header.
  • Network In / Network Out — traffic flowing to and from the service.
  • Healthy Instances — running copies vs. the target line.
  • Restarts — a flat line at zero is a healthy service.
  1. Use the 1H / 6H / 24H / 7D selector in the status banner to change the window for every chart.
  2. If the service runs more than one instance, a dropdown appears in the section header — switch between All instances · aggregate and each individual instance to spot an unhealthy one.
  3. Hover any chart to read exact values at a point in time.

images.alt: Resource Metrics charts for CPU, memory, network, healthy instances and restarts
images.caption: Resource Metrics: CPU, memory, network, instances and restarts — with a per-instance dropdown

images.alt: Resource Metrics charts for CPU, memory, network, healthy instances and restarts
images.caption: Resource Metrics: CPU, memory, network, instances and restarts — with a per-instance dropdown

steps.title: Check third-party AI providers
steps.content:
Your agents also depend on outside AI services — and iMash watches those too. Scroll to the bottom of the Overview and find the Third-Party Providers section:

  • Each row shows a provider (such as OpenAI, ElevenLabs, Cartesia or Google Gemini API) with its current status pill.
  • The 30-day history bar shows one block per day — hover a block for that day's details.
  • Click the link icon (or Check status) to open the provider's own official status page.
⚠️ If your voice agent suddenly sounds off or responds slowly while all iMash services are green, check this section — a degraded LLM or TTS provider is the usual culprit, and it typically recovers on the provider's side.

That's it — you now have a single page that tells you, at any moment, exactly how the platform powering your business is doing.

images.alt: Third-Party Providers section with OpenAI, ElevenLabs, Cartesia and Google Gemini API status history
images.caption: Third-party AI providers with 30-day status history and links to their official status pages

images.alt: Third-Party Providers section with OpenAI, ElevenLabs, Cartesia and Google Gemini API status history
images.caption: Third-party AI providers with 30-day status history and links to their official status pages

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials
